children.text: Documenting the days of District past
We are currently rebuilding our site. This section in particular requires some work to add additional content and photos, to existing and new articles.  Please revisit for new content.
If you have any content you would like to share, please send to:

mainImage.alt: Black and white photo in a book
mainImage.caption: 2003-2004 squad

seo.description: Photos and stories of the District.

children.text: Today, Orpington District played Potters Bar. I thought they were an excellent team. Before we played the match we had breakfast, our whole team were really energetic as we really needed to win the game (Editors note: Nice but not entirely vital!) as it would have been our first win in Jersey. We won 4-1. After the match Orpington and the Pink Ladies (Managers for the day), who have managed 19, won 15 drawn 4 and lost 0, went over to cheer the Red team on. On the bus home everyone was happy and celebrating all the way back to the Hotel. Personally, I, Roman Hall, thought we played well.

Today we went into the hotel pool after the game the outdoor pool was really cold. Adam swam the whole length in the cold pool. We was all ecstatic because we won 4-1 and our first win of the tournament. When was in the hotel pool we all jumped in at once and made a huge splash. We was in the pool for a long time over 45 minutes. As soon as we got back to our rooms we ate lunch I had pizza I ate pretty quick. After Lunch we went to Aquasplash. So it was even more swimming there was many water slides and diving boards, there was a lazy river as well furthermore a very big wave machine. When we were at Aquasplash there was this girl who had a crush on Roman and Daniel and she kept following us around the lazy river. When we all went down the slide I got stuck in the middle. So I had to jump off and run. Also I bumped into my mum on the street the day was probably the best day and at the end of the day we won 4-1.

seo.description: Today, Orpington District played Potters Bar. I thought they were an excellent team.

children.text: After a very nice breakfast we set off on the minibus to play Plymouth A. A team which beat Gloucester A. We watched and supported the Blue team play Potters Bar B. They won 4-1. After the blue team finished their game it was our turn to play the game. Unluckily Pave got injured so Josh had to go in goal. We raised the bar even higher. I think we played the best football we have ever played for district. After a great team performance by everyone. Matt and Kai both scored brilliant headers and Joe C scored a cracker after a great cross from Alfie. When the ref blew the final whistle we were all proud of our performance. We got the first win in Jersey and it was against an A side.

After a great game against Plymouth. We had a trip to the best Zoo I have ever been to. First of all we saw giant bears eating the grass and then the family of Gorillas. We even saw an eye-eye which was thought to be extinct, people even thought it was a cat, a squirrel, or even a lemur. We saw Otters eating fish and another otter just taking the food out of its mouth with his own hands. When we finished exploring we went to a little shop. I bought my sister something for her, some eggs (chocolate) and seagull poop which was raisins in chocolate. When we got back we had a brilliant dinner. Bavdzo się tiátuj podabato. By Spiderman

seo.description: After a very nice breakfast we set off on the minibus to play Plymouth A. A team which beat Gloucester A.

children.text: After yesterdays frustrating draw against Newbury. Everyone woke up raring to go. Yesterday evening we discussed how to beat Barking (a physical team) everyone’s hopes were high to earn our first victory.

One hour later, we arrived in minibuses at our football pitch. We quickly got changed and started to warm up. We knew it was going to be a hard game. However we had a plan: our tactic was to put them under pressure from kick off, we looking for a crucial win. A couple of minutes into the game they scored a deserved but lucky goal. Despite conceding early we bounced back with a curling cross from Daniel and finished by a header from Tom. once again we conceded a scrappy goal from a corner. 2-1 down after 10 minutes. The goals were rushing in! From Barking’s good spell of play they scored a stunning long range effort. The Orpington fans cheered us on as we du deep. We found ourselves dominating which soon paid off when Jack finished our fast attack. We kept the pressure on but could not score before halftime. Ben demanded us to carry on what we were doing in the late stages of the first half. (Editors note: asked politely!) We tried to break down the stubborn Barking defence but we couldn’t. Attack after attack. In the closing stages Barking scored a heartbreaking header. From then on we didn’t create too many chances and consequently we lost 4-2.
Following lunch and hard match, Blue squad went Gerald Durrells Zoo for endangered animals. We were all split into groups of 3 and 4 with 1 adult to supervise us.
Our main challenge was to decode and find letters for a word to do with the zoo. Each different letter was dotted around the Zoo in different places.

One of my favourite parts was visiting the exact spot that Jambo protected the little boy from being tossed around and pulled to shreds. Also in the Gorillas habitat I liked watching the male and female Gorillas fight for their son. The son still oblivious to the situation and swinging rope to rope. An amazing day was had by everyone and all would definitely come back again.

seo.description: After yesterdays frustrating draw against Newbury. Everyone woke up raring to go. Yesterday evening we discussed how to beat Barking (a physical team) everyone’s hopes were high to earn our first victory.

children.text: Today we lost against Gloucester A. The score (7-0) did not reflect on how we played. After the match we went swimming in the hotel pool. It was great fun when we played Water Rugby. We then had Lunch a choice of a Jumbo sausage or Spag Bol. After lunch we were invited to play the Jersey U10 development squad and for the first time on this tour we kept a clean sheet and won 3-0.
We then went to Green Bay and there was some amazing rocks to climb especially for Pavel (Spiderman). We then headed back for dinner, it was delicious. After dinner we headed back for an early night.

seo.description: Today we lost against Gloucester A. The score (7-0) did not reflect on how we played.

children.text: On Tuesday 7th April Orpington B played Potters Bar A. Unfortunately we lost 4-0 but we didn’t stop trying and pot 100% effort in and just could not squeeze a goal in. After the game against Potters Bar we had mild chicken curry and apple crumble for lunch. 30 minutes later Orpington went to Aqua Splash. Aqua Splash is a very enjoyable water park. After having fun at Aqua Splash, we took a walk down to Costcutter to buy some sweets for our hotel.

For our dinner we had Duck breast and fudge cake. At 9 o’clock some of us went to the beach, we ran past a speed check and the majority of us got 16 miles per hour. On our journey back we was all really tired, when we reached the hotel we put our head on the pillow and shut our eyes.

seo.description: On Tuesday 7th April Orpington B played Potters Bar A. Unfortunately we lost 4-0 but we didn’t stop trying and pot 100% effort in and just could not squeeze a goal in.
